mission.title: kubescape: Default upload of scan results should be opt-in
mission.description: Default upload of scan results should be opt-in. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check Kubescape installation
steps.description:
Verify your Kubescape installation:
```bash
kubescape version
```
Install if needed: `curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubescape/kubescape/master/install.sh | /bin/bash`. Kubescape can run as a CLI tool or be deployed in-cluster.

steps.title: Review kubescape configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubescape configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape
kubectl get configmap -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubescape
```
I want to preface this issue as, I think `kubescape` is a pretty nice and nifty tool. It's especially useful for finding misconfigurations in k8s clusters, pods, containers, etc. But, I also want to say that `--results-locally` should not be opt-in.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Default upload of scan results should be opt-in
steps.description:
Hi   and thank you for your feedback.

As i'm replying to you I'm working on fixing this issue, in the mean time you can follow the instructions provided [here](https://github.com/armosec/kubescape/issues/193#issuecomment-951343897)

If you do not have the framework cached, you can download the
```yaml
> kubescape scan framework nsa --exclude-namespaces kube-system,kube-public --use-from /tmp/nsa.json
>
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape
kubectl get events -n kubescape --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Default upload of scan results should be opt-in" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi   and thank you for your feedback.

As i'm replying to you I'm working on fixing this issue, in the mean time you can follow the instructions provided [here](https://github.com/armosec/kubescape/issues/193#issuecomment-951343897)

If you do not have the framework cached, you can download the [release](https://github.com/armosec/regolibrary/releases/tag/v1.0.79) running ->
```
curl -L
codeSnippets:
▶ kubescape scan framework nsa --exclude-namespaces kube-system,kube-public
error: failed to download file, status code: 403 Forbidden
ARMO security scanner starting
[progress] Downloading/Loading framework definitions
kind: Framework, name: nsa, error: failed to download file, status code: 403 Forbidden

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubescape installed or the issue environment reproducible.
